element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Practice mode with real-time feedback; changes to suspension, turbo, anti-roll bars directly affect handling
No car damage; unlimited testing laps with timing; learn track layout and optimal setup configuration
Car damage enabled; 4-lap session with mean time determining grid position; introduces time pressure and mechanical consequences
No car damage; no yellow flags; full-speed racing with 32 AI competitors; learn racing lines and positioning
Speed restrictions (~90 mph); no passing allowed until incident cleared; introduces traffic management and patience
Wheel damage reduces control; engine damage is unrecoverable; impacts pit strategy and risk management
All mechanical systems active; AI retirements from failures; fuel/pit strategy; endurance management
6 camera angles including crash replay; review previous 20 seconds; post-race learning tool

typical_relief_triggers: Completion of practice lap with improved setup
Yellow flag period (forced safety reprieve at reduced speed)
Finishing position confirmed at race end
typical_tension_triggers: AI car crashes on track (debris hazard, yellow flag)
Entering turn with traffic ahead at high speed
Approaching pit sequence in close race
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Graduated difficulty curve: safe practice environment builds to qualifying pressure, then race tension scales with distance (10→30→60→200 laps). Tension modulated by yellow flag periods and pit stop mechanics. Long-format races sustain tension across extended time periods with mechanical attrition threatening retirement.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Practice mode establishes safe learning environment; unlimited time to optimize car setup with immediate feedback. First-person perspective only. No consequences for mistakes.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Qualifying introduces car damage and time pressure; player must balance aggressive performance against mechanical durability. Short race formats (10-30 laps) reduce commitment while maintaining stakes.
difficulty_curve.late_game: 60-lap and 200-lap races demand sustained performance with mechanical attrition. Full damage model requires pit strategy. AI cars exhibit retirements from random failures, creating dynamic field compression. Yellow flag management becomes critical. Winning requires endurance pacing rather than pure speed.
notable_spikes: Transition from practice to qualifying (damage enabled first time)
30-lap race introduction (yellow flag system and damage persist)
200-lap race (full mechanical failures on AI; extends tension across 60+ minutes of gameplay)

notable_level_patterns.description: Structured sequence: car setup freedom in practice → competitive pressure in qualifying → graduated race distances (10/30/60/200 laps) with progressively enabled systems. Each step introduces one new constraint or consequence.

Car damage system introduced only after player demonstrates competency in shorter races. Damage types escalate: single wheel (recoverable)→multiple wheels (difficult)→engine (terminal). Random AI mechanical failures add unpredictability only in longer formats.

Short races skip yellow flag logic entirely; intermediate races introduce yellow flags with traffic management; full 200-lap race combines yellow flags with mechanical attrition requiring dynamic race reading.
